data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron Get FDA Approval for Dupixent to Treat Rare Skin Disease
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Sanofi and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals said they got Food and Drug Administration approval for anti-inflammatory drug Dupixent as a treatment for a rare skin disease, adding an eight indication in the U.S. for their blockbuster medicine.

France's Sanofi and Tarrytown, N.Y.-based Regeneron said Friday that the FDA gave green light for Dupixent as a treatment of adult patients with bullous pemphigoid, a skin disease that mainly affects elderly people and is characterized by itch, blisters and lesions, as well as a reddening of the skin.

The approval was based on the results of a pivotal study that showed more patients on the drug achieved sustained disease remission and itch reduction than those on placebo, the companies said. The FDA evaluated the drug under priority review, a process for medicines that could bring significant improvements to the treatment of serious conditions, they added.

Sanofi and Regeneron jointly developed Dupixent, which generated more than 13 billion euros ($14.95 billion) in sales last year, under a global collaboration agreement.

The latest FDA authorization means the drug is approved in the U.S. to treat eight different diseases, including skin, gut, and respiratory-system conditions, that share an underlying type of inflammation, they said.

Additional regulatory applications are under review around the world, the companies said.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: Vopak Unit to Invest EUR53 Million in New Ammonia Storage Terminal in India
data.description:
Koninklijke Vopak's 42.23%-owned unit AVTL will invest 53 million euros to develop a new brownfield ammonia storage terminal in Pipavav, India.

The terminal will offer a storage capacity of 36,000 metric tons and will aid in the import and export of ammonia, the Dutch tank storage company said Friday.

The commissioning of the terminal is anticipated by the end of 2026.

data.title: Yaoko Considers Cutting Investment Unit To Broaden Investor Base
data.description:
Yaoko Co.,Ltd said it is considering a reduction in its investment unit to expand its investor base and improve stock liquidity, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The supermarket chain said it will weigh factors such as stock price trends, market conditions, shareholder composition, and trading volumes when deciding on the timing and method of any potential changes.

No specific plans or schedule have been determined.

The disclosure follows Tokyo Stock Exchange rules requiring companies with investment units exceeding 500,000 yen to report their stance on potential reductions.

data.title: Shenzhen New Industries Secures Approval for CO₂ Testing Kit
data.description:
Shenzhen New Industries Biomedical Engineering obtained medical device registration for its CO₂ Assay Kit from Guangdong's medical products administrator.

The kit is a diagnostic tool to assess acid-base imbalances in blood, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The approval for the Class II medical device is valid until June 17, 2030.

Shares of the biomedical technology company were down over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Labubu Maker's Shares Drop as Chinese State Media Signal Tighter Scrutiny
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Shares in Pop Mart International, the maker of the wildly popular Labubu dolls, took a hit after Chinese state media signaled stricter regulation over "blind-box" toys.

Pop Mart's Hong Kong-listed shares fell as much as 6.2% Friday morning before paring losses.

That followed a commentary by The People's Daily, the official newspaper of the Chinese Communist Party, calling for more regulation of so-called blind box collectables, which customers buy without knowing what's inside.

Pop Mart's success has been built on blind-box products, which have won fans worldwide who collect a range of characters that the company either licenses or designs in-house.

One of these, Labubu--an elf with pointy ears, devilish eyes and a cheeky grin--has become a global sensation in recent months, thanks in large part to celebrities showing off their dolls.

Noting the recent popularity of blind-box toys among children and teenagers in China, The People's Daily article called for tighter regulation to protect minors from impulse purchases.

It's not the first time such concerns have been voiced.

China's State Administration for Market Regulation, a market watchdog, proposed guidelines for blind boxes in 2023, banning their sale to children under eight to prevent minors from becoming addicted.

Beijing took action to address similar concerns about videogames a few years ago, aiming to prevent gaming addiction and unsupervised in-game purchases. That led to time limits and restrictions on when minors could play videogames, part of a sector-wide crackdown that wiped billions of dollars in value from China's gaming giants.

Concerns about the impact of tighter regulation on Pop Mart may be overdone, said Morningstar analyst Jeff Zhang. Given that the company's customer base demographic is largely 18 and above, the downside seems limited, he said.

"Additionally, we think that a key driver of its growth is likely to come from its overseas markets, so any regulatory clampdown in its domestic market might not pose a significant challenge on its overall growth," he said.

Toy companies that cater more to younger customers stand to be more affected, said Zhang.

Friday's decline is a drop in the bucket for Pop Mart's red-hot stock, which remains up over 160% so far this year.

Last year, when the Labubu craze began, Pop Mart's shares surged 340%, making it one of the best-performing stocks in Hong Kong. The Beijing-based company's profit nearly tripled for the year, while revenue more than doubled.

This year, Pop Mart expects revenue to grow over 50% and overseas sales to more than double.

For now, the Labubu frenzy shows no signs of slowing. Earlier this month, a human-size Labubu sold for over $150,000 at a Chinese auction house, reflecting the continued appeal of "ugly-cute" doll.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Man Infraconstruction Forms New Subsidiary
data.description:
Man Infraconstruction has incorporated a subsidiary, named MICL Shreepati August, to deal in the real estate space, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company's capital contribution in the new company is 50,500 Indian rupees, amounting to a 50.5% partnership interest in MICL Shreepati.

The company's shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

ArcelorMittal

Steelmaker ArcelorMittal dropped plans to convert two plants in Germany to carbon-neutral production because the country's energy plans were too high.

The French state will become Eutelsat's biggest shareholder after a 1.35-billion euro capital increase intended to help the satellite company compete with Elon Musk's Starlink.

Dutch parcel deliverer PostNL placed on Thursday a Schuldschein of 100 million euros, comprising maturities of 3 and 5 years with both fixed and floating interest rates

Abu Dhabi's Ministry Of Health announced on Thursday a partnership with Sanofi to accelerate vaccine development.

data.title: NTT Completes Tender Offer For NTT Data With 337 Million Shares Tendered
data.description:
Nippon Telegraph and Telephone Corp acquired 336.8 million shares of NTT Data Group Corp through a tender offer that closed on June 19, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The offer, launched on May 9, was priced at 4,000 yen per share.

NTT had aimed to acquire up to 592.8 million shares and required a minimum acceptance of 125.3 million shares. The final tendered amount exceeded that threshold, allowing NTT to purchase all tendered shares.

The tender offer is part of NTT's plan to increase its ownership in NTT Data, one of Japan's largest IT services companies.

data.title: India Shares Set for Weekly Gains
data.description:
The BSE Sensex rose 387 points, or 0.5%, to 81,752 in early trade on Friday, halting losses from the previous two sessions.

The Nifty 50 also climbed 0.5% to trade above 24,900.

The gains followed news that US President Donald Trump postponed a decision on U.S. involvement in the Israel-Iran conflict by two weeks.

All sectors traded in the green, led by financials, healthcare, and auto.

Small-cap and mid-cap indices also advanced, rising 0.4% and 0.3%, respectively.

However, traders remained cautious ahead of the monetary policy meeting minutes due later today, looking for clues on the future interest rate path amid easing inflation.

Among early gainers were Mahindra & Mahindra (2.5%), Bharti Airtel (1.7%), Power Grid (1.6%), Reliance Industries (1.1%), and Nestle India (1.0%).

For the week, the index is on track for a 0.8% gain, recovering from losses in the previous week, supported by strong domestic economic data.

data.title: Chung Fu Tex-International Shares Rise 3% After Court Backs Land Sale Proposal
data.description:
Shares of Chung Fu Tex-International rose about 3% in recent trade after it received two civil rulings from Taiwan's Intellectual Property and Commercial Court.

The rulings dismissed shareholder Li-Chung Huang's petitions to block a land disposal proposal at its upcoming annual meeting, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The shareholder sought to halt discussions and voting on a June 20 proposal to sell all land at the company's Zhongli Plant, citing insufficient disclosure and potential legal violations

However, the court found Chung Fu had adequately informed shareholders about the transaction, including its method, rationale, and minimum tender price.

The company does not expect the ruling to impact its operations or financials.

data.title: Political uncertainty weighs on Thai baht; Asia currencies see weekly losses
data.description:
Key points:
  • Thai baht set for worst week since Feb-end
  • Indonesian rupiah set for worst week since early-April
  • Philippine peso poised for biggest weekly drop since October
  • KOSPI tops 3,000 mark for first time since Jan 2022

The Thai baht weakened on Friday and was set for its worst weekly performance in four months as political turmoil further dampened risk sentiment that was already shaken by the escalating Middle East conflict and trade worries.

The baht was down for a fifth consecutive session, last trading down 0.1%. It was on course to log its worst week since late February.

Thai stocks rose 0.6%, after falling 2.4% in the previous session.

Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra's government was on the brink of collapse after a leaked phone call with Cambodia's former leader led to Bhumjaithai Party's withdrawal from the alliance, while other coalition partners reconsidered their support.

The developments come as Thailand continues to see bond outflows due low returns and more vulnerability to U.S. trade tariffs, even as other emerging Asian economies attract capital due to a shift away from American assets.

"At a time when unprecedented geoeconomic challenges are confronting Thailand, the lack of political leadership/certainty is an unwelcome handicap for trade/diplomacy and wider geopolitical leverage," said Vishnu Varathan, head of macro research for Asia excluding Japan at Mizuho Bank, in a note.

"The BoT (Bank of Thailand) arguably needs to compensate, hastening another 50-75bp of rate cuts," he said adding that the optimal policy-mix for the central bank would be to cut rates, while stabilizing the baht.

Other regional currencies inched up on the day but were set for weekly losses. The dollar index was poised for a 0.5% weekly climb as worries about the escalating Israel-Iran conflict and potential involvement of the United State fuelled an appetite for traditional safe havens.

The South Korean won rose 0.8% but was set for its second consecutive weekly fall. The Indonesian rupiah was on course for its worst weekly performance since early-April.

Meanwhile, the Hong Kong dollar hit the weak end of its trading band for first time in two years.

The Philippine peso was last flat but was poised for its biggest weekly drop since October. The country's central bank delivered a widely expected 25 basis point rate cut on Thursday and left the door open for at least one more trim this year.

Taiwan's dollar rose 0.3%, a day after the country's central bank kept its policy rate unchanged and signalled there was no need for a rate cut this year. Taipei stocks fell 0.3%.

Most other stock markets rose on the day, with Seoul shares rising 1.2% to cross the 3,000 mark for the first time since January 2022. Malaysian and Indian equities added 0.2% and 0.4%, respectively.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** China keeps benchmark lending rates unchanged as expected in June

** Thailand foreign tourist arrivals so far this year fall 3.59% y/y

** Japan's core inflation hits 2-year high, keeps rate-hike bets alive

data.title: Seven Bank Buys Back 194 Million Shares, Ends Parent Tie With Seven & i
data.description:
Seven Bank repurchased 194 million shares for 50.82 billion yen through the Tokyo Stock Exchange's ToSTNeT-3 off-auction trading system on Friday, according to a same-day filing on the local bourse.

The buyback completes a program approved by the board on June 19 to purchase up to 200 million shares for 52.4 billion yen, equivalent to 17% of the company's outstanding stock.

Following the repurchase, Seven Bank's parent, Seven & i Holdings , cut its stake to 39.9% from 46.4%, ending Seven Bank's status as a consolidated subsidiary.

data.title: Relief Therapeutics Receives FDA Response to QIDP Request for RLF-TD011
data.description:
GENEVA, SWITZERLAND / ACCESS Newswire / June 20, 2025 / RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA (SIX:RLF)(OTCQB:RLFTF)(OTCQB:RLFTY ) (Relief or the Company), a biopharmaceutical company committed to delivering innovative treatment options for select specialty, unmet and rare diseases, today announced that its initial request for Qualified Infectious Disease Product (QIDP) designation for RLF-TD011 for the treatment of epidermolysis bullosa (EB) was not granted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The FDA determined that the investigational therapy does not currently meet the criteria for QIDP designation.

This outcome does not impact the Company's development and regulatory strategy for RLF-TD011. The program continues to benefit from both Orphan Drug and Rare Pediatric Disease designations, which provide important regulatory and commercial incentives, including the potential for seven years of market exclusivity in the United States upon approval. RLF-TD011 is further supported by the Company's intellectual property portfolio, including a recently granted European patent providing protection through 2040.

The Company also reported that it held a constructive pre-Investigational New Drug (pre-IND) meeting with the FDA. Discussions focused on the remaining clinical and regulatory steps required to advance RLF‑TD011 into later-stage development. Relief expects to complete the pre-IND meeting process upon receipt of the FDA's written responses in the coming weeks.

ABOUT EPIDERMOLYSIS BULLOSA

Epidermolysis bullosa (EB) is a group of rare, inherited connective tissue disorders characterized by extreme skin fragility, leading to blistering and wounds from minor friction or injury. In severe cases, blisters can develop into chronic wounds or form in internal organs such as the mouth or esophagus, leading to painful wounds, recurrent infections, and a deeply impacted quality of life. EB is classified into several major inherited subtypes, each defined by the depth of blister formation within the skin's layers: epidermolysis bullosa simplex (EBS), dystrophic epidermolysis bullosa (DEB), junctional epidermolysis bullosa (JEB) and Kindler syndrome (KS). Treatment is intensive and includes wound care, infection prevention, and pain management. Approximately 500,000 individuals worldwide are affected by EB.

ABOUT RLF-TD011

RLF-TD011 is a highly pure, stabilized hypochlorous acid solution developed using Relief's proprietary TEHCLO™ technology. With strong antimicrobial properties, RLF-TD011 is a sprayable, self-administered solution for targeted wound application while avoiding skin contact and cross-contamination. Relief's acid-oxidizing solution has previously demonstrated efficacy in accelerating wound closure and reducing infections in certain clinical trials on non-EB wounds. In an investigator-initiated trial (NCT05533866), RLF‑TD011 has also shown promising results in infection control and wound healing in EB patients with the most severe forms of the disease. RLF-TD011 aims to address unmet needs in EB care by efficiently controlling infection and inflammation while reducing antibiotic use and easing the intensive, time-consuming wound care routine required by current treatments. The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted RLF-TD011 both Orphan Drug and Rare Pediatric Disease designations for EB.

ABOUT RELIEF

Relief is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to advancing treatment paradigms and improving the lives of patients with rare and debilitating diseases. With core expertise in drug delivery systems and drug repurposing, Relief's clinical pipeline includes innovative treatments designed to address critical unmet medical needs in rare dermatological, metabolic and respiratory conditions. The Company has also successfully brought several approved products to market through licensing and distribution partnerships. Headquartered in Geneva, Relief is listed on the SIX Swiss Exchange under the symbol RLF and quoted in the U.S. on OTCQB under the symbols RLFTF and RLFTY. For more information, visit www.relieftherapeutics.com

CONTACT

RELIEF THERAPEUTICS Holding SA

Jeremy Meinen

Chief Financial Officer

contact@relieftherapeutics.com

DISCLAIMER

This press release contains forward-looking statements, which may be identified by words such as "believe," "assume," "expect," "intend," "may," "could," "will," or similar expressions. These statements are based on current plans and assumptions and are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results, financial condition, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Such factors include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, market developments, regulatory changes, competitive dynamics, and other risks or changes in circumstances. This communication is provided as of the date hereof, and Relief undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements contained herein as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

SOURCE: Relief Therapeutics Holding SA

data.title: Koa Shoji Holdings To Receive 697 Million Yen Dividend From Subsidiary
data.description:
Koa Shoji Holdings will receive dividends around 697.0 million million yen from its consolidated subsidiary Koa Shoji Co. Ltd. on June 23, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The dividend will be recorded as operating revenue in the company's non-consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ending June 2025.

The payment will not affect consolidated results as it will be eliminated through intercompany accounting.

data.title: Sakai Chemical Buys 500,000 Shares Via ToSTNeT-3 Buyback
data.description:
Sakai Chemical Industry repurchased 500,000 shares for 1.32 billion yen through the Tokyo Stock Exchange's ToSTNeT-3 off-auction trading system on Friday, according to a same-day filing on the local bourse.

The transaction, at 2,644 yen per share, was part of a buyback program authorized on May 14, allowing the purchase of up to 1 million shares, or 6.2% of outstanding stock, for a maximum of 2.5 billion yen through November 30.

After this off-auction buyback, Sakai Chemical plans to continue repurchases via market trading. All repurchased shares will eventually be cancelled.

Sakai Chemical shares gained nearly 1% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Private Sector Growth Slows While Labor Market Tightens, Inflation Stabilizes Amid Global Risks, NAB Says
data.description:
National Australia Bank said that recent economic data show slower private sector growth and softer spending, but a tighter labor market than expected, with unemployment gradually rising and inflation stabilizing within the Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) target range, according to a NAB report on Thursday.

NAB forecasts a 1.7% gross domestic product growth this year and around 2.25% next year, driven by stronger household spending, rising dwelling investment, and steadier business investment, with unemployment projected to rise to 4.4% later in the year before stabilizing around 4.25% by the end of 2026, the report said.

Household consumption growth slowed in the first quarter, and early second-quarter spending remained soft despite a May rebound, while real disposable income stays below pre-pandemic levels, signaling ongoing challenges for consumer demand.

Despite steady employment growth in the first quarter amid weather challenges, cautious hiring and weak business conditions continue due to profitability pressures, although trade is expected to recover following a slow start, the filing added.

CoreLogic's dwelling prices rose 0.5% in May compared with 0.2% in April, and first-quarter residential investment grew despite falling approvals and supply challenges.

New dwelling price increases have boosted inflation forecasts, while expectations remain that the RBA will implement three more rate cuts this year amid slower consumption, global uncertainties, and a tight labour market.

data.description:

data.title: Palm Oil Below MYR 4,100 but Heads for Strong Weekly Rise
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil prices traded slightly below MYR 4,100 per tonne, remaining subdued for a second straight session as traders awaited export estimates from cargo surveyors.

They also assessed reports that top buyer India had canceled 65,000 metric tons of crude palm oil orders scheduled for delivery between July and September, following a sharp rally in prices.

Still, palm oil contracts are on track for a fifth consecutive weekly gain, up around 4% so far, supported by expectations of stronger demand in the coming months.

In the broader energy market, oil prices are heading for solid weekly gains, as tensions between Israel and Iran persist with no signs of de-escalation.

Meanwhile U.S. President Trump is expected to decide within two weeks whether the U.S. will intervene, keeping markets on edge.

data.title: EIB to allot 70 bln euros for tech sector in 2025-2027-officials
data.description:
The European Investment Bank is likely to announce on Friday plans to pump 70 billion euros into the development of European technology firms over the next three years, EU officials said.

The programme, called Tech EU, is meant to help Europe compete with China and the United States in the race for innovative clean and digital technologies.

The EIB, the biggest multilateral lender in the world with a balance sheet total of 556 billion euros, expects its own 70 bln euros to mobilise a further 250 billion euros of private cash as investors crowd into projects supported by the EIB, EU officials said.

The 70 billion is to be split into 20 billion euros for equity and quasi-equity, 40 billion euros for loans and 10 billion for guarantees in 2025-2027, the officials said.

The plan is to complement European Commission efforts to support higher risk ventures and innovative companies throughout their investment journey, from proof of concept to an initial public offering.

The EIB wants to focus on supercomputing, artificial intelligence, digital infrastructure, critical raw materials, green industries such as offshore wind, health, security and defence technologies, robotics and advanced materials, the officials said.

data.title: Sanofi Obtains US FDA Approval for Dupixent as Treatment for Bullous Pemphigoid
data.description:
Sanofi said Friday it secured approval from the US Food and Drug Administration for Dupixent, or dupilumab, as a treatment for adults with bullous pemphigoid, a chronic, rare skin condition.

The approval was based on data from the phase 2/3 Adept study, which assessed the efficacy and safety of Dupixent compared with placebo. In the study, 18.3% of the patients achieved sustained disease remission and 38.3% experienced clinically meaningful itch reduction, versus 6.1% and 10.5% for placebo, respectively.

The French biopharmaceutical company also applied for Dupixent's regulatory approval in other markets, including the European Union, Japan, and China.

data.title: Maps Says Shareholder Eiffel Investment Group Crossed 10% Threshold, Now Holds 10.25% Of Share Capital
data.description:
Maps SpA :

Meanwhile, the Hong Kong dollar hit the weak end of its trading band for first time in two years.

The Philippine peso was last flat but was poised for its biggest weekly drop since October. The country's central bank delivered a widely expected 25 basis point rate cut on Thursday and left the door open for at least one more trim this year.

Taiwan's dollar rose 0.3%, a day after the country's central bank kept its policy rate unchanged and signalled there was no need for a rate cut this year. Taipei stocks fell 0.3%.

Most other stock markets rose on the day, with Seoul shares rising 1.2% to cross the 3,000 mark for the first time since January 2022. Malaysian and Indian equities added 0.2% and 0.4%, respectively.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** China keeps benchmark lending rates unchanged as expected in June

** Thailand foreign tourist arrivals so far this year fall 3.59% y/y

** Japan's core inflation hits 2-year high, keeps rate-hike bets alive

data.title: SinoPac Financial's Shares Slip 1% After FSC OKs Conversion of King's Town Bank into Unit
data.description:
Shares of SinoPac Financial slid 1% in recent trade after the company said it received regulatory approval from Taiwan's Financial Supervisory Commission (FSC) to convert King's Town Bank into a unit through a share swap in a late Thursday bourse filing.

The companies will decide and announce the effective date of the share swap at a later time, the filing said.

data.title: Guangdong Golden Dragon Development Actual Controller's Stake Drops to 4%
data.description:
Guangdong Golden Dragon Development's actual controller Yang Zhimao's stake declined to 4.0% from 7.4% following a judicial auction, which led to the transfer of 30.3 million shares originally held by Yang.

The shares were acquired by three individuals via Taobao's judicial auction platform and formally transferred on June 17, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the financial services provider slipped 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Italgas Reports 98.7% Of Shares Offered Subscribed For EUR 1 Bln At End Of Rights Issue Subscription Period
data.description:
Italgas SpA :

  • SAID ON THURSDAY IT HAD CONCLUDED SUBSCRIPTION PERIOD OF RIGHTS ISSUE

  • RIGHTS FOR SUBSCRIPTION EXERCISED FOR AROUND 98.7% OF TOTAL NEW SHARES OFFERED, TOTAL PRICE OF EUR 1 BLN

  • SHAREHOLDER CDP RETI SUBSCRIBED TO 52,684,606 NEW SHARES, TOTAL CONSIDERATION OF EUR 264.7 MLN

  • REMAINING RIGHTS TO BE OFFERED ON ITALIAN STOCK EXCHANGE AT JUNE 23, 24, UNLESS EARLY CLOSURE

  • UNEXERCISED RIGHTS ENTITLE TO SUBSCRIBE AT PRICE OF EUR 5.025/NEW SHARE, RATIO OF 1 NEW SHARE FOR EVERY 4 UNEXERCISED RIGHTS

Further company coverage:

data.title: Dear Life Acquires Tokyo Apartment Property
data.description:
Dear Life acquired an income-producing apartment property in Setagaya, Tokyo, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The five-story reinforced concrete building, tentatively named DeLCCS Komazawa-Daigaku, is located at a nine-minute walk from Komazawa-Daigaku Station, the filing said.

The property has a floor area of 783.6 square meters.

Dear Life funded the acquisition using internal funds and bank borrowings. The seller and purchase price were not disclosed due to confidentiality agreements.
